_doc: Composition-blind food-form adjustments to the per-food FII tag (src/core/foodAdjust.ts), keyed by USDA fdcId. Some inflammatory signals are invisible to a food's nutrient composition — fermentation / live-culture content and food structure chief among them — so a small additive delta on the −2 (anti) … +2 (pro) axis corrects the per-food tag AFTER the composition FII, then re-clamps to ±2. Negative = nudged more anti-inflammatory. Each delta's DIRECTION is evidence-based (cited); its MAGNITUDE is a deliberate calibration value, like the energy mass floor, not a biomarker-derived constant. Applied by both the engine (src/core/score.ts) and the rescorer (scripts/score-recipes-inflammation.mjs); mirrored arithmetic in scripts/lib/fii.mjs. Seeded with fermented dairy: yogurt is consistently anti-inflammatory in trials, yet the composition FII reads it as pro-inflammatory twice over — its saturated fat and sodium, and its intrinsic milk lactose, which the fibre-based free-sugar estimate (fii-parameters.json) penalises because dairy carries no fibre to 'cover' it (WHO free sugars exclude milk lactose). The yogurt delta (−1.3) corrects both mis-reads; aged cheese gets a smaller delta (−0.4) since its high sodium is a genuine pro-inflammatory signal that fermentation only partly offsets. The layer generalises: it can later hold other food-form signals (e.g. ultra-processing).

171284.reason: plain whole-milk yogurt — fermented dairy anti-inflammatory beyond composition; corrects intrinsic-lactose over-penalty
